Taily Video Production HQ — Team Brief

PUH-BrianMartell-T235-Lead-3 Resolution Note (Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20288075, May 19, 2026) cleanly resolves the final major open sub-question from the Third Law Lead Note. Core Result: PUH’s geometric mechanism (240-fold E₈ lattice connectivity preventing thermal isolation) predicts a universal N^(-1/3) cooling-rate ceiling for macroscopic systems of N Planck cells. This comes from the boundary-to-volume ratio: heat escapes only through the surface (~N^(2/3) cells) while the bulk has N cells. Standard quantum mechanics predicts no such universal scaling — its limits depend on system-specific quantum coherence and de Broglie wavelength. The conceptual distinction is clear (geometric lattice connectivity vs. quantum zero-point fluctuations), but practical testing is currently impossible: real experiments are ~35 orders of magnitude below the PUH theoretical ceiling due to mundane engineering limits (heat leaks, bath coupling, etc.), not fundamental physics. Both frameworks agree on current data.
